commit.message:
refactor: remove regex legal ref check, let LLM handle it

The regex legal ref whitelist kept causing false positives:
- 'Legge 78/2010' blocked because whitelist has 'DL 78/2010' (type prefix mismatch)
- 'L 207/2024' blocked because normalization stripped the dot

The LLM fact-check correctly identifies truly fabricated laws (e.g., D.Lgs 147/2015
for tassa salute) while understanding that type prefix variations of real laws aren't
fabrications. Regex pre-filter still catches fake institutions, acronyms, and known
hallucination patterns.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Enable runtime-async in CoreCLR System.Private.CoreLib (#126594)

## Description

Runtime-async was enabled for `src/libraries` (non-mobile/wasm) but was
missing from the CoreCLR `System.Private.CoreLib`. This adds the
`runtime-async=on` feature flag to
`src/coreclr/System.Private.CoreLib/System.Private.CoreLib.csproj`,
matching the existing NativeAOT pattern with architecture and runtime
flavor exclusions:

- **riscv64** excluded per #124934
- **loongarch64** excluded per #124935
- **Mono flavor** excluded (covers browser/wasm, wasi, android, iOS,
etc.)

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="'$(TargetArchitecture)' != 'riscv64' and '$(TargetArchitecture)' != 'loongarch64' and '$(RuntimeFlavor)' != 'Mono'">
  <Features>$(Features);runtime-async=on</Features>
</PropertyGroup>
```

---------

* Removed unused components and layouts from the project

Moved `SagraFacile.Web` up one folder.

* Updated `.gitignore` to exclude artifacts folder.

* Refined localization setup, enhanced culture fallback, and adjusted "Now Calling" header formatting.

* fix: eliminate Blazor Server DbContext concurrency crashes via factory pattern, optimistic locking, and UI guards (#25)

* fix(concurrency): use IDbContextFactory + transient repos to prevent Blazor Server threading crash

Replaces AddDbContext<ApplicationDbContext> with AddDbContextFactory<ApplicationDbContext>
and changes all three repository registrations from Scoped to Transient.

Each repository now creates its own private DbContext via the factory in its constructor
and disposes it via IAsyncDisposable. This ensures that every mediator handler call —
including concurrent SignalR callbacks — gets a completely isolated DbContext instance
with no shared change-tracker state between parallel operations.

AddDbContextFactory still registers ApplicationDbContext as a scoped service so
ASP.NET Identity stores continue to work unchanged.

Infrastructure tests are updated: TestDbContextFactory now implements
IDbContextFactory<ApplicationDbContext> and all repository test files pass the factory
directly to repository constructors, using `await using` for proper disposal.

Co-authored-by: PrinceEntree8 <32601364+PrinceEntree8@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/PrinceEntree8/SagraFacile/sessions/a3ca2d5b-f06e-4aa2-8414-14643d3951f7

* feat(concurrency): add Version concurrency token to TableReservation for optimistic locking

Adds a long Version column to TableReservations that is auto-incremented by
ReservationRepository.SaveChangesAsync for every modified TableReservation entity.
EF Core includes this in the WHERE clause of UPDATE statements so any concurrent
write that has already incremented the version is detected and results in a
DbUpdateConcurrencyException, which the repository translates to a clean
RepositoryConcurrencyException from the Application.Exceptions namespace.

CallReservation, SeatReservation, and VoidReservation handlers now catch
RepositoryConcurrencyException and return a user-friendly failure Result instead
of letting the exception propagate and crash the Blazor circuit.

A custom RepositoryConcurrencyException is defined in the Application layer so
the Application project stays free of any direct dependency on EF Core
(clean-architecture boundary preserved).

New migration AddTableReservationConcurrencyToken adds the Version bigint column
(default 0) to the existing TableReservations table.

Three new unit tests verify that each handler converts a concurrency exception
to a failure result with an appropriate message.

Co-authored-by: PrinceEntree8 <32601364+PrinceEntree8@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/PrinceEntree8/SagraFacile/sessions/a3ca2d5b-f06e-4aa2-8414-14643d3951f7

* fix(ui): add consistent operation locks across Events, Receptionist, and HeadWaiter pages

Events.razor:
- Add activatingEventId field to track which event is being activated
- Disable the Activate button for the specific event during the async operation
- Use try-finally to guarantee the lock is always released even on exception

Receptionist.razor:
- Extend the New Reservation submit button's disabled expression from
  @isCreating to @(isCreating || actionInProgress != null) so that the
  form cannot be submitted while a Call or Void action is already running
  on a row in the table, preventing accidental duplicate submissions

HeadWaiter.razor:
- Extend the Announce button's disabled expression from @isAnnouncing to
  @(isAnnouncing || actionInProgress != null) so the button is also
  locked while a Seat operation is in progress, preventing conflicting
  state updates when the announce triggers a SearchBestFit refresh

commit.message:
fix: deactivate LVM/dm before partitioning; partprobe after --force --no-reread

Root cause of 'no space left on device' install failure:
- Disks previously used for LVM keep the disk open via the device-mapper layer
- sfdisk falls back to --force --no-reread (kernel rejects BLKRRPART)
- Without --no-reread recovery, the kernel retains the OLD partition table
- mkfs then formats the wrong-sized (old) partition → 930 GiB disk gets
  a 1 GiB root filesystem → bootc fills it instantly during install

Two fixes:
1. unmountAll() now calls deactivateLVM() to tear down LVM VGs and DM
   devices (LUKS, dm-crypt) backed by the target disk before sfdisk runs.
   This allows sfdisk's first attempt to succeed without --force.
2. After the --force --no-reread retry path, partprobe + udevadm settle
   is called to force the kernel to re-read the new partition table before
   any mkfs operations run.

Adds regression test: TestPartition_PartprobeAfterForceReread verifies
that partprobe is called after the --force sfdisk retry.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: pre-clean Python 3.14 MSI leftovers before installing

Previous failed attempts leave JustForMe package registrations in the
MSI database. When the bundle migrates them to AllUsers, it uninstalls
the JustForMe packages AFTER installing AllUsers ones - both to the same
TargetDir - wiping the stdlib and causing 'Could not find platform
independent libraries' on first python.exe run.

Fix: enumerate and msiexec /x all Python 3.14 components before the
fresh install so the bundle sees a clean slate.

Validate version on release (#22)

Fixes https://github.com/github/spark/issues/2259

Our typical release process for this component is just to set a tagged
release and let that go. This has the downside that our binary also
thinks it knows its version over in cmd/version.go.

Rather than do something elaborate when we're not releasing this often,
this PR simply adds a guardrail that will fail the release if the
versions don't align. In that case, the move is to remove the partial
release/tag, commit the updated version, and then release.

This should be sufficient for the moment, and if we get working on this
more often to where it's an annoyance, we can put a more thorough system
in place.

feat(palette-builder): add actionable accessibility fix suggestions in theme arranger

When a text/bg or accent/bg pairing fails WCAG contrast thresholds, the
theme arranger now shows an actionable suggestion instead of a text hint:
- A color swatch showing the suggested fix color
- The exact hex value
- A label describing the fix (e.g. 'lighter text', 'darker accent')
- An 'apply' button that immediately swaps the color into that role

findAccessibleVariant() steps a color's HSL lightness until it meets the
target ratio (7:1 for AAA text, 4.5:1 for AA). For dark mode it tries
lightening the foreground first, then darkening the background as fallback.
For light mode the directions are reversed.

commit.message:
refactor: simplify fact-check to 3 layers (regex+LLM+test)

Remove fragile regex heuristics (factCheckNumbers, assertNoFabricatedStatistics)
that kept causing false positives. Promote llmFactCheck to primary blocking gate
with multi-model fallback, structured verification prompt covering all 7 categories
(laws, institutions, rates, statistics, dates, source coherence, invented facts),
lower blocking threshold (0.6), and fail-safe when all verification models unavailable.

Architecture: L1=assertNoFabricatedReferences (fast regex pre-filter) →
L2=llmFactCheck (primary gate, GPT-4.1→GPT-4o→Gemini Flash fallback) →
L3=article-fabrication-guard.test.ts (permanent safety net)

commit.message:
fix(seo): add expired data for Artisa Group job to fix canonical→listing

The job 'segretaria-assistente-progetto-artisa-group-lugano' was removed
from active data but never archived to expired, causing its soft-landing
page to have minimal content. Google's canonicalization then chose the
listing page instead of the self-referencing canonical.

This adds proper expired entries with translated titles, descriptions,
and salary data so the build plugin generates enriched soft-landing pages
that Google will respect as canonical.

Root cause: the Artisa crawler removed the job from its slice but the
daily cleanup-jobs.mjs had not yet run to archive it. The compat-paths
pipeline still generated a page, but without expired metadata the content
was too thin for Google to treat as a distinct page.

commit.message:
fix(eoc-crawler): prevent slug churn from location instability

EOC jobs accumulated 10+ previousSlugs each (958 total), bloating the
build with thousands of redirect pages. Root causes:

1. Location instability: extractEocLocation() produces different cities
   across crawl runs (Bellinzona→Novaggio→Lugano) depending on which
   hospital name appears first in the description. The base crawler's
   merge step treats location changes as genuine slug changes.

2. No cap on previousSlugs: every slug change accumulated indefinitely.

Fixes:
- Add stabilizeEocSlugsInMemory(): compares post-merge jobs with
  pre-crawl slice data by vacancy ID. If the title is unchanged, any
  slug change is location-driven and gets reverted.
- Add previousSlugs capping (MAX_PREVIOUS_SLUGS=5) in
  postProcessEocJobsInMemory(): deduplicates against current slugs,
  then keeps only the 5 most recent entries.
- Cap existing data: 958→660 previousSlugs (31% reduction).

Tests: 12 tests pass (5 new covering stabilization + capping).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
